figures.caption: Figure 2: Illustration of gather-based versus broadcast-based execution for one layer.

Figure 4: Time taken (left Y axis, hatched bars for extrapolated, solid bars for completed runs) and extrapolated data read from disk (right Y axis, marker) for complete execution of Ginex and layer-wise executions of DGI and ATLAS.

Figure 6: Performance comparison of OG, RND, and AT orderings showing read (reload) and write (eviction) times (bars, left Y axis), avg. % destination vertices reloaded per chunk (red circles, inner right Y axis), and total execution time (green stars, outer right Y axis).

Figure 7: Performance comparison of RND, LRU, and AT eviction policies. Top Row: Coldstore read (reload) and write (eviction) times (bars, left Y axis), avg. % destination vertices reloaded per chunk (red circles, inner right Y axis), and total execution time (green stars, outer right Y axis). Bottom row: Cumulative reload distribution of the number of unique vertices reloaded from the cold store.
Table 1: Graph datasets [11,14] used in experiments.

Figure 1: Time taken (left Y axis, hatched bars : extrapolated, solid bars: completed) and Total bytes read from disk (markers, right Y axis), for full-graph inference of 2-layer SAGEConv, with topology and features disk-resident for Ginex [24], DGI [44], Marius [34] and ATLAS (ours), for Papers [11] and MAG240M-Cites [11], on 4090 and 5090 GPU workstations.

Figure 8: Sensitivity of ATLAS to hot-store memory budgets for IL and MA. Top Row: Execution time (bars, left Y axis) and num. reloads (marker, right Y axis) across memory budgets. Bottom row: Num. vertices in cold store state across chunks.

Figure 5: Resource utilization for a 2-layer GCN on the IL and MA datasets. First row: CPU (blue, left Y axis) and memory (red, right Y axis) over time. Second Row: GPU util (sky blue, left Y axis) and memory (red, right Y axis) usage over time. Third row: SSD read (green, left Y axis) and write (orange, right Y axis) bandwidth over time. Last row: Baseline “read-only” microbenchmark vs. ATLAS active read rate.
